sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Zachary, presents with right elbow pain, which he claims started since yesterday, but later corrects that it is actually his left arm that hurts.
He describes the pain as achiness and heaviness, which affects his entire left arm from the elbow to the neck.
He denies any specific incident that may have caused the pain, such as lifting heavy objects.
He has taken some Motrin for the pain, but it has not provided significant relief.
He also mentions that the pain is accompanied by chest pain, which he describes as coming and going, affecting his neck as well.
He denies shortness of breath.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he is on 2.5mg of Norvasc, and diabetes, for which he is on 500mg of metformin.
His A1C result from a previous visit was 7.2.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms besides the elbow and chest pain.
Current medications: Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension, Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90, which is slightly high.
Physical exam: The patient's physical exam reveals a slight murmur with his heart, which was not noted previously.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with symptoms suggestive of a possible myocardial infarction (heart attack).
Problem list: Hypertension; Diabetes; Suspected myocardial infarction; Chest pain; Elbow pain.
Plan:
I prescribed Aspirin and Nitroglycerin to help alleviate the chest pain.
I will get an EKG to further evaluate the patient's heart.
I will also perform a glucose test to assess the patient's blood sugar levels and adjust his medication as needed.
The patient will be kept on 500mg of metformin for diabetes and 2.5mg of Norvasc for hypertension.
The patient will be referred to the hospital for further evaluation and treatment of the suspected myocardial infarction.
Follow-up: The patient will be admitted to the hospital for further evaluation and treatment.
